mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and boosting consumer spending power, which lifts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent alongside rising retail sales and consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in 3 out of 4 calendar years, or forward returns turn negative when both conditions align simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during a post-shock recovery phase, indicating supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvements that directly boost corporate revenue forecasts and equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple recovery episodes, or the signal reverses direction during supply-side bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising by more than 100,000 units month-over-month acts as a leading indicator for broader construction and consumer confidence, lifting cyclical equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Housing starts spikes fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns in at least 70% of observed instances, or the relationship breaks down during credit crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls increasing by over 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand shock, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 3-year period, or forward returns are indistinguishable from market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer credit balances indicate expanding household purchasing power, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS momentum shows no statistically significant predictive power for 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple decades, or the signal consistently predicts negative returns when sales rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the CPI inflation rate, real interest rates turn positive, increasing the discount rate for equities and compressing valuation multiples, which drives negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive real rates fail to produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or equity valuations expand despite sustained real rate hikes.
